title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser presents a technique for sustaining software shipping with minimal human attention by dispatching agents to isolated git worktrees via voice briefs and managing them remotely. The system layers signal agents monitoring Slack and Linear, verification gates including lint and browser interaction, weekly JSONL history analysis for skill generation, and biometric feedback integration via MCP.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS demonstrates a workflow for continuing to ship software with minimal hands-on time, dispatching agents to isolated git worktrees through voice briefs and managing them remotely. The system stacks signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates such as lint and browser interaction, weekly analysis of JSONL history to generate skills, and biometric feedback routed through MCP. The takeaway is a concrete architecture for offloading routine development work to agents while keeping checks in place to catch failures.
